retag_note: Expanded 2026-05-04 from MECH-303 to [MECH-303, MECH-304] following the MECH-303/304 split. Standard safety signal (vmPFC/hippocampus) maps to MECH-303; conditioned inhibitor (dorsal striatum/dlPFC) maps to MECH-304. Both claims directly supported.

source.title: Cortico-Striatal Activity Characterizes Human Safety Learning via Pavlovian Conditioned Inhibition

confidence_rationale: 7T human fMRI study dissociating conditioned inhibitor (actively predicts threat omission when paired with known threat signal) from standard safety signal (passively unpaired with threat). Post-split, this paper directly supports both MECH-303 and MECH-304: the standard safety signal substrate (vmPFC + hippocampus) maps to MECH-303's contextual-passive claim; the conditioned inhibitor substrate (dorsal striatum + dlPFC) maps to MECH-304's cue-specific teaching-signal-driven claim. This is the load-bearing dissociation paper that motivated the MECH-303/304 split.
failure_signatures: Post-split the conditioned inhibitor substrate (dorsal striatum + dlPFC) supports MECH-304 rather than undermining MECH-303. However, the fMRI design cannot distinguish learning-phase encoding from retrieval/expression, so it remains possible that the striatal signal reflects retrieval of a stored prediction rather than the update-step encoding that MECH-304 posits.

The study used healthy human participants. The vmPFC/hippocampus finding for standard safety signals may reflect retrieval or expression of safety rather than the encoding/updating of the predictive store. These are confounded in the blocked fMRI design.
No single-unit resolution; cannot distinguish encoding from retrieval, or distinguish safety-prediction updating from safety-prediction retrieval expression.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Conditioned inhibition (actively predicting threat omission) engages dorsal striatal cortico-striatal circuitry; standard safety signals (CS-) engage vmPFC + hippocampus. These two forms of safety learning dissociate at the neural level in humans.
mapping.ree_translation: MECH-303 posits a prefrontal-hippocampal substrate (vmPFC + hippocampus) for contextual passive safety encoding. The vmPFC + hippocampus finding for standard safety signals directly supports this. MECH-304 posits a cortico-striatal substrate (dorsal striatum + dlPFC) for cue-specific conditioned inhibition driven by a teaching signal (MECH-302). The dorsal striatum + dlPFC finding for conditioned inhibitors directly supports MECH-304. The within-subjects dissociation in this paper is the empirical justification for registering MECH-303 and MECH-304 as separate claims with anatomically distinct substrates.
mapping.mapping_caveat: The conditioned inhibitor vs. standard safety signal distinction was the motivation for splitting MECH-303 into MECH-303 (contextual/passive/vmPFC-hippocampal) and MECH-304 (cue-specific/teaching-signal/cortico-striatal). Post-split the mapping is cleaner but residual caveats remain: blocked fMRI cannot separate encoding from retrieval/expression, and the paradigm involves Pavlovian threat-cue conditioning rather than harm-stream suppression as REE posits.
mapping.source_context: 49 healthy human participants, ultra-high field 7T fMRI, Pavlovian conditioned inhibition paradigm, two types of safety signal contrasted within-subjects.

confidence_components.notes: High source quality (7T fMRI, n=49, whole-brain corrected, peer-reviewed J Neurosci). Mapping fidelity is lower because the conditioned inhibitor vs standard safety signal distinction cuts across MECH-303's claim in a way the claim does not anticipate. The human fMRI setting means low rodent-to-human transfer risk, but high encoding-vs-retrieval confound risk.
